text:
Irrelevant and redundant features are a common factor leading to the decline of model performance, and thus the decline of detection accuracy. In this paper, a hybrid method named IGRRF-E (Improved Greedy Relief – Recursive Feature Elimination) is proposed to improve the accuracy of multi-class network anomaly detection based on multilayer perceptron (MLP) network.

IGRF-RFE integrates the advantages of the filter and the wrapper methods in a computationally efficient manner. The IGRF-RFE algorithm applies two phases of feature selection. In the first phase, an ensemble of two filter criteria, Information Gain (IG) and Random Forest (RF) feature importance, are used to filter out the irrelevant and unimportant attributes. The RF criterion is very beneficial because it reduces the bias of IG towards high frequency but low-importance features, and only keeps high importance features for further analysis. In the second phase, a wrapper criterion based on the actual performance of an MLP model is used with the recursive feature elimination (RFE) criterion to select the final set of features. RFE criterion is very useful because it not only selects the important features but it also shows the relationship between the features and the model that they are selected for.

Introduced a novel feature selection approach, IGRF-RFE. Experiments are performed on UNSW-NB15 dataset. Some preprocessing techniques such as removing duplicated instances and class resampling are introduced to prevent the ranking bias and overfitting. The experimental results demonstrated that IGRF-RFE can effectively reduce the number of features from 42 to 23. Meanwhile, the accuracy of the multi-class classification of the MLP increased from 82.25% to 84.24%, thereby improving the efficiency of network intrusion detection.
explanation: The passage is highly structured and domain-specific, which can occur in both human and AI outputs; however, the inconsistent acronym usage (IGRF-E vs IGRF-RFE) and unusually polished phrasing suggest possible AI-generated text.
